channel.title: Bullet Proofing on Blake Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Bullet Proofing on Blake Blog

item.title: 📖Toc
item.description: Focus on feeling it, if you don’t feel it it’s not working

item.title: Ankle
item.description:
Resist Ankle movement with hand - then eventually use body weight - can use band or something else for harder


Bodyweight to add resistance

Passively: you can just hold it in a stretch

item.title: Baseline
item.description:
ATG Baseline
One
Two

Hits all the joints/whatever but maybe just from different angles/methods - stretching vs rotational/radio vs flow thingy

item.title: Hip Stability
item.description:
Pidgeon squat - and then into dragon/whatever that progression is
hip airplane
stair leg movement
kettlebell workouts from guy featured on squat university
One legged deadlift
one side loaded deadlift
farmers carries
Overhead squat
Butt walk things

with all of these you have to maintain perfect posture for them to work as that’s what you what you want the muscles to fight for

Stability - planche, iron cross, l-sit rope climb, rope climb

item.title: Hips
item.description:
**Need to do the PT tests each week and monitor changes (then monthly and so on)
Fix pain as it arises and work on stability when not in pain (progressive overload very very slowly and safely - volume of exercises not so much weight)
Hip Tests
Journal

⭐️Swimming + standing desk
⭐️Maybe the spruhts
Laying hip pull down thing then glute bridge
Hip airplanes

Issues
Pirifornis, hip flexors + weak glute leading to anterior tilt and performs pushing on sciatic nerve causing pain
Something in shoulder pushing on nerve causing impingement - then on that gets relieved from the neck stretch

item.title: Hips Published
item.description:
**Need to do the PT tests each week and monitor changes (then monthly and so on)
Fix pain as it arises and work on stability when not in pain (progressive overload very very slowly and safely - volume of exercises not so much weight)
[Hip Tests](#Hip Tests)

Basic 80-20 Solver for Most Hip Issues

This is a basic 80/20 solver for most hip issues (especially for the squat) should be done every other day, before leg, days, or whatever frequency works best for you, very easy on the joints
12 Minute Hip Mobility Routine - Tom Merrick

item.title: Low Back
item.description:
Good Mornings and Reverse Hyper are your big two, ask the front desk about reverse hyper machine
Then stability/abs
Then bridge just for safety/coolness (maybe morning routine) Morning routine - sun salutation, pidgeon/lizard, worldest greatest stretch, bridge (then bridge reach, until you can do a backbend+back hamstring)

Cut out bent over rows
GHD if they don’t have the Nordic curl machine (it’s like the same thing)

Bridge
Good Mornings
QL Raise
Should pres thing
RDLs
BRACE - this is your biggest safety net, work on stability (Hip airplane, lateral load)+ AB Rollouts to get this better - practice this hard when you workout (actively think about it)
⭐⭐⭐Reverse Hypers (done at the end of deadlift or leg day)
Mc Gill big 3/ Ab wheel+L-sit routine

item.title: Shoulder
item.description:
3-Step “Reverse Out Shoulder Pain” Routine - long form version, short summary is fine
Shorts summary version (same title) - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s57DUHHfWGs

Each exercise done 3 x 8-12
In practice I could only do half of a first set, then 2nd and 3rd, it ends up being like 2 sets are just fine - weight should start at none and just work up slowly, you will get sore, of course use enough weight that you feel it working, nut if you have existing injury take additional caution until you build it back up and regain your safety
The way down is more important than the way up - controlling of the weight and moving it well


Rest your elbow right on your quad or elbow whatever is comfortable
Fight it down, dead stop at bottom, explode up (when you’re able to), that is our safety goal where we know we can control the load
no motion in the body other than rotation
Goal: 10% body weight, EX. 20 lbs at 200 bodyweight


Lower over 3-5 seconds
this is going to be hard, pay attention to how his knees are place to position yourself correctly
You’ll have to play with what angle the bench should be at
Slowly slowly load

item.title: Shoulder Published
item.description:
[Rotator Cuff](#Rotator Cuff Strengthening)
[Weird Tingly](#Rotator Cuff Strengthening)

Nerve Glide/Entrapment
This exercise can serve to fix this feeling and also serve as a test to determine what is happening

For any impingement in your joints you first want to
Test - to see what range(s) of motions are suffering and determine your approach to either
Lengthen - a lack of mobility is causing issue
Strengthen - an imbalance is causing issue(either across the same joint like the hip or along the chain)
